---
title: "nitrix.stats.lme.reml"
generated: true
signature-hash: 2fa9be29438b703b
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.lme.reml`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `2` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `r` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `nt` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `q` | random-effect columns |
| `L` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `REMLResult` {#REMLResult}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REMLResult -->
```python
class REMLResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:REMLResult -->
Per-voxel REML fit output.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:REMLResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(theta_hat: Float[Array, 'V 2'], beta_hat: Float[Array, 'V p'], log_lik: Float[Array, 'V'], fixed_cov: Float[Array, 'V p p'], theta_cov: Float[Array, 'V 2 2'], grad_m: Float[Array, 'V 2 p p'], blups: Optional[Float[Array, 'V q']] = None) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `LMEResult` {#LMEResult}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LMEResult -->
```python
class LMEResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:LMEResult -->
Block-Woodbury (R2) mixed-model fit output -- one correlated / diagonal
random slope `(1 + x | g)` / `(x || g)`.

The `lme_fit` dispatcher returns this for the R2 tier; the scalar-intercept
R1 tier returns a :class:`REMLResult` (the FaST-LMM path), and the nested /
crossed / structured-residual tiers their own result types.

Carries the fixed-effect contrast-inference tensors (`fixed_cov` /
`theta_cov` / `grad_m`, audit D3-R2), so `lme_t_contrast` /
`lme_f_contrast` accept it with the **same** Satterthwaite / Kenward-Roger
df machinery as R1.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LMEResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(beta_hat: Float[Array, 'V p'], cov_re: Float[Array, 'V r r'], sigma_e_sq: Float[Array, 'V'], log_lik: Float[Array, 'V'], fixed_cov: Float[Array, 'V p p'], theta_cov: Float[Array, 'V nt nt'], grad_m: Float[Array, 'V nt p p'], tier: str, blups: Optional[Float[Array, 'V q r']] = None) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `NestedLMEResult` {#NestedLMEResult}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:NestedLMEResult -->
```python
class NestedLMEResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:NestedLMEResult -->
Nested two-level mixed-model fit output (the `lme_fit` R3 path).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:NestedLMEResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(beta_hat: Float[Array, 'V p'], var_outer: Float[Array, 'V'], var_inner: Float[Array, 'V'], sigma_e_sq: Float[Array, 'V'], log_lik: Float[Array, 'V'], tier: str) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `CrossedLMEResult` {#CrossedLMEResult}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:CrossedLMEResult -->
```python
class CrossedLMEResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:CrossedLMEResult -->
Crossed two-factor mixed-model fit output (the `lme_fit` R4 path).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:CrossedLMEResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(beta_hat: Float[Array, 'V p'], var_group: Float[Array, 'V'], var_cross: Float[Array, 'V'], sigma_e_sq: Float[Array, 'V'], log_lik: Float[Array, 'V'], tier: str) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `LMEContrast` {#LMEContrast}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LMEContrast -->
```python
class LMEContrast(NamedTuple)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:LMEContrast -->
Per-voxel mixed-model t-contrast result.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `LMEFContrast` {#LMEFContrast}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LMEFContrast -->
```python
class LMEFContrast(NamedTuple)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:LMEFContrast -->
Per-voxel mixed-model F-contrast result.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `reml_fit` {#reml_fit}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:reml_fit -->
```python
reml_fit(Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], X: Float[Array, 'N p'], Z: Float[Array, 'N q'], *, theta_init: Optional[Float[Array, 'V 2']] = None, n_iter: int = 20, damping: float = 1e-06, block: Optional[int] = None, low_rank: Optional[bool] = None) -> REMLResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:reml_fit -->
Voxelwise variance-components REML fit.

Fits the LME

.. code:
    y_v = X beta_v + Z b_v + eps_v
    b_v ~ N(0, sigma_b^2 I_q)
    eps_v ~ N(0, sigma_e^2 I_N)

independently for each voxel, sharing the fixed-effect design
`X` and the random-effect design `Z` across voxels.  Uses
the FaST-LMM spectral trick: eigendecompose `ZZ^T` once,
rotate `y` and `X` into the diagonalising basis, then
Newton-score on the variance components in log-space.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:reml_fit -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` × `N` | — | Response tensor, `(V, N)` -- `V` voxels, `N` subjects (or observations). |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` × `p` | — | Fixed-effect design, `(N, p)`. Shared across voxels; carries its own intercept column (no intercept is added). |
| `Z` | `Float` | `N` × `q` | — | Random-effect design, `(N, q)`. Shared across voxels. The random-effect covariance is `sigma_b^2 I_q` (single variance component); pass each component's design matrix column to `Z` to model multiple random effects (currently all share `sigma_b^2`; for separate variance components per random effect, the lower-level `_varcomp` core accepts a general `(K, N)` basis). |
| `theta_init` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'V 2']]` | — | `None` | Per-voxel initial log-variances, `(V, 2)`. Defaults to a heuristic split of empirical variance. |
| `n_iter` | `int` | — | `20` | Fixed Newton-scoring iteration count. Default `20` -- typically converges in 5-10 for well-conditioned data. |
| `damping` | `float` | — | `1e-06` | Levenberg-Marquardt-style damping on the average-information matrix. Default `1e-6`; raise if Newton steps are unstable near boundaries. |
| `block` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Optional voxel-block size: cap the number of voxels whose per-Newton intermediates are live at once, bounding peak memory on brain-scale `V`. `None` (default) is a single `vmap` over all voxels (identical numerics and HLO to before). |
| `low_rank` | `Optional[bool]` | — | `None` | Use the FaST-LMM **low-rank** decomposition: a `q x q` eig of `Z^T Z` (`O(N q^2 + q^3)`) instead of the dense `N x N` eig of `ZZ^T` (`O(N^3)`). Requires `q <= N` (the usual tall random-effect design); the `N - q` null directions enter only through per-voxel Gram aggregates, and a rank-deficient `Z` (e.g. phantom one-hot columns from gappy labels) is absorbed by that null space. **Default `None`** auto-selects: low-rank when `q < N` (the brain-scale case), dense otherwise. Pass `True` / `False` to force; the two match to the iterative tolerance. |

**Returns** `REMLResult`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen perf:reml_fit -->
:::: {.callout-note title="Benchmarks — nitrix-perf-bench" collapse="true"}
GPU steady-state **11.52 ms** (median, L4).  [full report](https://github.com/hypercoil/nitrix-perf-bench/blob/main/reports/PERF_REML_FIT.md)

::: {.table-responsive}
| case | platform | param | baseline | status | steady (min/med) | compile | mem | fidelity | ratio |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| reml_fit | jax-cpu | V=1024,k=8,n=24 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 150.62 ms / 155.65 ms | 1.290 s | 693 MB (rss) | ✓ 0.31×tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cpu | V=1024,k=8,n=24 | `statsmodels.MixedLM` | ok | 11.268 s / 11.432 s | 12.844 s | 559 MB (rss) | ✓ 0.057×tol | 74.81x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cuda12 | V=1024,k=8,n=24 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 11.29 ms / 11.52 ms | 2.001 s | 135.03 MB (hbm) | ✓ 0.12×tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cuda12 | V=1024,k=8,n=24 | `statsmodels.MixedLM` | skipped | — | — | — | — | — |
| reml_fit | jax-cpu | V=256,k=8,n=24 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 49.98 ms / 82.08 ms | 1.752 s | 674 MB (rss) | ✓ 0.29×tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cpu | V=256,k=8,n=24 | `statsmodels.MixedLM` | ok | 2.920 s / 2.925 s | 3.526 s | 558 MB (rss) | ✓ 0.055×tol | 58.41x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cuda12 | V=256,k=8,n=24 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 11.35 ms / 11.50 ms | 2.193 s | 151.20 MB (hbm) | ✓ 0.11×tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cuda12 | V=256,k=8,n=24 | `statsmodels.MixedLM` | skipped | — | — | — | — | — |
| reml_fit | jax-cpu | V=64,k=8,n=24 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 12.80 ms / 14.67 ms | 1.172 s | 673 MB (rss) | ✓ 0.16×tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cpu | V=64,k=8,n=24 | `statsmodels.MixedLM` | ok | 703.84 ms / 723.15 ms | 2.069 s | 557 MB (rss) | ✓ 0.04×tol | 55.01x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cuda12 | V=64,k=8,n=24 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 11.30 ms / 11.64 ms | 3.041 s | 151.05 MB (hbm) | ✓ 0.065×tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| reml_fit | jax-cuda12 | V=64,k=8,n=24 | `statsmodels.MixedLM` | skipped | — | — | — | — | — |
:::

*source: bench `d7e2dfb8` · nitrix `039cf43e`*
::::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `lme_fit` {#lme_fit}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:lme_fit -->
```python
lme_fit(Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], X: Float[Array, 'N p'], *, group: Int[Array, 'N'], z: Optional[Float[Array, 'N r']] = None, inner: Optional[Int[Array, 'N']] = None, cross: Optional[Int[Array, 'N']] = None, corr: Optional[Union[str, CorrSpec]] = None, time: Optional[Float[Array, 'N']] = None, structure: Structure = 'unstructured', n_iter: int = 20, damping: float = 1e-06, block: Optional[int] = None, low_rank: Optional[bool] = None, retain_blups: bool = False) -> Union[REMLResult, LMEResult, NestedLMEResult, CorrLMEResult, CrossedLMEResult]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:lme_fit -->
Voxelwise mixed model, dispatched to the cheapest exact solver.

A single grouping factor `group` with per-observation random covariates
`z` (`None` -> a scalar random intercept).  The dispatcher honours the
**performance-preserving** invariant (v3 §0.1): it routes to the cheapest
solver that is *exact* for the given random structure, so the shipped fast
paths stay the realised path for the cases they already serve.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:lme_fit -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` × `N` | — | `(V, N)` responses and `(N, p)` shared fixed-effect design. `X` carries its own intercept column (no intercept is added). |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` × `p` | — | `(V, N)` responses and `(N, p)` shared fixed-effect design. `X` carries its own intercept column (no intercept is added). |
| `group` | `Int` | `N` | — | `(N,)` integer group labels (`0 .. M-1`). The **outer** factor `g1` when `inner` is given (R3). |
| `z` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N r']]` | — | `None` | `(N, r)` per-observation random covariates; `None` is the scalar random intercept (`r = 1`, `z = 1`). |
| `inner` | `Optional[Int[Array, 'N']]` | — | `None` | `(N,)` **nested** inner factor `g2` (each `(group, inner)` pair is a sublevel `g1:g2`). When given, routes to the R3 nested solver (`z` must be `None`); `None` (default) keeps the single-factor R1/R2 dispatch. |
| `cross` | `Optional[Int[Array, 'N']]` | — | `None` | `(N,)` second **crossed** factor. When given, routes to the R4 crossed solver -- `(1 \| group) + (1 \| cross)`, the two factors crossed (not nested). `z` / `inner` / `corr` must be unset. Tier-2; cost scales with the *smaller* factor's level count cubed. |
| `corr` | `Optional[Union[str, CorrSpec]]` | — | `None` | Within-group residual correlation structure (`'ar1'` / `'car1'` / `'cs'` or a :class:`CorrSpec`). When given, routes to the R2 + corr whitened block-Woodbury (a random effect plus a structured residual); `None` (default) is the `sigma_e^2 I` residual. |
| `time` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N']]` | — | `None` | `(N,)` observation times for a `car1` residual (see `corr`). |
| `structure` | `Structure` | — | `'unstructured'` | `'unstructured'` (full `r x r` `G`, `(1 + x \| g)`) or `'diagonal'` (independent variance components, `(x \|\| g)` -- the off-diagonal of `G` is held at zero). Both run the R2 block-Woodbury. |
| `n_iter` | `int` | — | `20` | Newton iterations, AI damping, and voxel-block chunking (all tiers). |
| `damping` | `float` | — | `1e-06` | Newton iterations, AI damping, and voxel-block chunking (all tiers). |
| `block` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Newton iterations, AI damping, and voxel-block chunking (all tiers). |
| `low_rank` | `Optional[bool]` | — | `None` | **R1-only.** Toggles the FaST-LMM q-rank decomposition on the single-scalar-factor (R1) path; `None` (default) auto-selects low-rank when `q < N` (the brain-scale case; matches the dense fit to tolerance). **Ignored on the R2 / R3 / R4 / +corr tiers**, which have no dense-vs-low-rank choice -- passing it there is a silent no-op. |
| `retain_blups` | `bool` | — | `False` | — |

**Returns** `Union[REMLResult, LMEResult, NestedLMEResult, CorrLMEResult, CrossedLMEResult]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `lme_t_contrast` {#lme_t_contrast}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:lme_t_contrast -->
```python
lme_t_contrast(result: 'Union[REMLResult, LMEResult]', contrast: Float[Array, 'p'], *, dof: ContrastDof = 'satterthwaite', X: Optional[Float[Array, 'N p']] = None, Z: Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']] = None) -> LMEContrast
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:lme_t_contrast -->
Per-voxel t-test of a mixed-model fixed-effect contrast `c^T beta = 0`.

The standard error is `sqrt(c^T (X^T V^{-1} X)^{-1} c)` from the fitted
`result.fixed_cov`; the denominator degrees of freedom are
**Satterthwaite** (the default, `dof='satterthwaite'`):

    df = 2 (c^T C c)^2 / (g^T Cov(theta_hat) g),
    g_k = d(c^T C c)/dtheta_k = w^T M_k w,   w = C c

using the per-voxel `theta_cov` / `grad_m` the fit already formed
(`_varcomp.varcomp_inference`).  cuSOLVER-free and differentiable.

`dof='kr'` instead applies the **Kenward-Roger** small-sample correction:
the adjusted covariance `Phi_A` (inflated for variance-component
uncertainty) and the scaled-`F` denominator df, with `t = sign(effect)
sqrt(F_KR)` on `df = m`.  KR rebuilds the rotated basis, so the original
design must be re-passed as `X=` / `Z=` (the compressed `REMLResult`
does not retain it); see :mod:`._kr`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:lme_t_contrast -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `'Union[REMLResult, LMEResult]'` | — | — | A :class:`REMLResult` (`reml_fit` / R1) **or** the R2 :class:`LMEResult` (`lme_fit` with a random slope) -- both carry the `fixed_cov` / `theta_cov` / `grad_m` inference fields the Satterthwaite df consumes (audit D3-R2). `dof='kr'` is R1-only. |
| `contrast` | `Float` | `p` | — | `(p,)` contrast vector `c` over the fixed effects. |
| `dof` | `ContrastDof` | — | `'satterthwaite'` | `'satterthwaite'` (default) or `'kr'` (Kenward-Roger, R1-only; pass `X` / `Z`). |
| `X` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N p']]` | — | `None` | The original fixed / random design -- required for `dof='kr'`. |
| `Z` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']]` | — | `None` | The original fixed / random design -- required for `dof='kr'`. |

**Returns** `LMEContrast`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `lme_f_contrast` {#lme_f_contrast}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:lme_f_contrast -->
```python
lme_f_contrast(result: 'Union[REMLResult, LMEResult]', contrast: Float[Array, 'L p'], *, dof: ContrastDof = 'satterthwaite', X: Optional[Float[Array, 'N p']] = None, Z: Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']] = None) -> LMEFContrast
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:lme_f_contrast -->
Per-voxel F-test of a multi-row mixed-model contrast `C beta = 0`.

For an `L`-row contrast matrix `C` (a single `(p,)` row is promoted to
`L = 1`), the Wald F-statistic is

    F = (C beta)^T (C Cov(beta) C^T)^{-1} (C beta) / L

with `Cov(beta) = (X^T V^{-1} X)^{-1}` from `result.fixed_cov`.  The
denominator degrees of freedom use the **Fai-Cornelius / lmerTest**
multivariate-Satterthwaite construction (`dof='satterthwaite'`, the
default):

1. eigendecompose `M = C Cov(beta) C^T = P diag(d) P^T` (a small `L x L`
   symmetric solve, cuSOLVER-free `sym_eig_jacobi`);
2. each eigendirection `l_m = (P^T C)_m` is an independent 1-df contrast
   with variance `d_m` and a per-direction Satterthwaite
   `nu_m = 2 d_m^2 / (g_m^T Cov(theta) g_m)`, `g_{m,k} = w_m^T M_k w_m`,
   `w_m = Cov(beta) l_m` (the same `M_k` / `Cov(theta)` the fit formed,
   `result.grad_m` / `result.theta_cov`);
3. combine by the E-method: `E = sum_m nu_m / (nu_m - 2)` over
   `nu_m > 2`, and `df2 = 2 E / (E - L)`.

For `L = 1` this collapses **exactly** to `lme_t_contrast` (`F = t^2`,
`df2` the t-Satterthwaite df, same p-value).  The F-statistic is
differentiable; the denominator df is not (the iterative eigensolver feeds it
under `stop_gradient` -- a model-selection-style scalar, per v3 §1.3).

`dof='kr'` instead applies the **Kenward-Roger** small-sample correction:
the adjusted covariance `Phi_A` and the moment-matched scaled-`F` (their
KR2), referred to `F(L, m)`.  It rebuilds the rotated basis, so re-pass the
original design as `X=` / `Z=` (the compressed `REMLResult` does not
retain it); `F = t^2` consistency with `lme_t_contrast(dof='kr')` holds
for `L = 1`.  See :mod:`._kr`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:lme_f_contrast -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `'Union[REMLResult, LMEResult]'` | — | — | A :class:`REMLResult` (`reml_fit` / R1) **or** the R2 :class:`LMEResult` (`lme_fit` with a random slope) -- both carry the `fixed_cov` / `theta_cov` / `grad_m` inference fields (audit D3-R2). `dof='kr'` is R1-only. |
| `contrast` | `Float` | `L` × `p` | — | `(L, p)` contrast matrix (or a `(p,)` single-row contrast). |
| `dof` | `ContrastDof` | — | `'satterthwaite'` | `'satterthwaite'` (default) or `'kr'` (Kenward-Roger; pass `X` / `Z`). |
| `X` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N p']]` | — | `None` | The original fixed / random design -- required for `dof='kr'`. |
| `Z` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N q']]` | — | `None` | The original fixed / random design -- required for `dof='kr'`. |

**Returns** `LMEFContrast`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
